lightHit: Is it athletic? Is it competitive? Then it's sport enough.
The IOC sells spectacle; this sells tickets.
heavyHit: You want tradition? The marathon started as a road race to a wedding.
Every 'not a real sport' takes exactly one generation to become one.
counter: Chess is an Olympic sport. Your line moved.
If archery counts, so does pixel-perfect aim.
block: Cute definition. Now defend why yours wins.
Keep the gate. I'll keep playing.
whiff: You described outdoor exercise. We were discussing competition.
That insult landed in the wrong game.
combo: Athlete, team, training, tournament, audience — check every box.
special: Rhetorical Tumble: the goalposts just relocated themselves.

lightHit: Rules are performances too. Watch who benefits.
Consistency is a costume hypocrites wear to parties.
heavyHit: Your universal principle has a 'except for me' clause written in invisible ink.
The saint and the scoundrel both quote the same rulebook.
counter: You called out inconsistency while wearing two faces.
The moral high ground is not a balcony; it is a treadmill.
block: Nice sermon. Is it for everyone, or just your enemies?
The point is sharp; the target is misplaced.
whiff: You indicted human nature. The question was about one choice.
That accusation needs a mirror, not a jury.
combo: Intent, rule, exception, beneficiary — pull the thread and the sweater confesses.
special: Rhetorical Tumble: your own rule just tripped you.

lightHit: Your grandmother's recipe already had borrowed spices.
heavyHit: Authenticity is just the first person to write the recipe down.
Fusion is not corruption; it is the history of flavor.
counter: You mourn tradition while eating a tomato — a New World immigrant.
Protection for whose tradition? The answer is always power.
block: Respect the source. Don't embalm it.
Cultural theft is real; this is not it.
whiff: You defended a cuisine that was itself a fusion.
That example is from a restaurant chain.
combo: Trade, migration, war, marriage — every dish is a map of contact.
special: Rhetorical Tumble: the purist's plate just got cosmopolitan.
